title: Decadent Irish Epoisses Bowl
description: A rich and creamy Irish dish featuring epoisses cheese, deep-fried to perfection and served in a warm dolsot stone bowl, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings.
instructions: In a shallow dish, mix together flour, salt, and a pinch of parsley.
In a separate dish, whisk together egg and buttermilk.
In a third dish, place the panko breadcrumbs.
Cut the softened epoisses cheese into 1-inch cubes and dip each cube into the flour mixture, then the egg mixture, and finally the breadcrumb mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Carefully place the breaded cheese cubes into the hot oil and fry until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the fried cheese from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
To assemble the bowls, place a few pieces of the fried cheese into a warm dolsot stone bowl, then sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
